title: Coccineorchis cernua
intro: Coccineorchis cernua, commonly known as the "red orchid," is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Coccineorchis cernua include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health and as a natural remedy for various infections.
